title: Implement configuration loading from TOML file and environment with validation
purpose: Implement the full configuration pipeline: define the ServerConfig type tree, parse TOML config files, overlay environment variables, and validate the merged result before the server starts. Configuration is the first thing the server does -- if it fails, the process exits with specific error messages. No listener binds, no cluster forms, no health endpoint starts until config validation passes. This is the declarative control plane: operators describe their topology in a config file, DevOps engineers override per-environment values via LIMINAL_ env vars, and the server rejects anything invalid before accepting a single connection.
task: Create the config module with four files. types.rs defines ServerConfig (listen address, channel definitions, routing rules, persistence paths, cluster config), ChannelDef (name, schema reference, durability mode), RoutingRuleDef, and ClusterConfig (seed nodes, node name). file.rs parses a TOML file path into a ServerConfig. env.rs scans for LIMINAL_ prefixed environment variables and overlays them onto a ServerConfig. validation.rs checks the merged config for completeness, type correctness, and reachability (e.g., persistence paths exist, listen addresses are parseable, channel names are unique). mod.rs declares sub-modules and re-exports the public API.

requirements.title: Define ServerConfig struct with all required fields
requirements.spec: ServerConfig SHALL contain fields for: listen_address (SocketAddr), channels (Vec<ChannelDef>), routing_rules (Vec<RoutingRuleDef>), persistence_path (Option<PathBuf>), and cluster (Option<ClusterConfig>). ServerConfig SHALL derive Debug and Clone. ServerConfig SHALL NOT implement Default -- every field is either required or explicitly Optional, with no silent defaults (P6). All fields SHALL be documented with their purpose.
acceptance: ServerConfig struct exists in crates/liminal-server/src/config/types.rs
ServerConfig has fields: listen_address, channels, routing_rules, persistence_path, cluster
ServerConfig derives Debug and Clone
ServerConfig does NOT implement Default
All fields have doc comments

files: crates/liminal-server/src/config/mod.rs:1-1 — currently empty placeholder; types.rs will be a new sibling and later re-exported from here.
crates/liminal-server/src/lib.rs:1-8 — crate root already exposes config and ServerError; keep public API reachable through liminal_server::config, no crate-root re-export required by brief.
crates/liminal-server/src/error.rs:1-10 — existing ServerError variants used by later parsing/validation code; public config types should avoid introducing a separate error type.
docs/design/server/briefs/SRV-002.json:14-23 — exact ServerConfig fields, derives, doc-comment acceptance criteria.
context: ServerConfig does not exist yet; config/mod.rs is a one-line empty file, so this requirement is a clean add in config/types.rs.
Workspace lints deny unwrap/expect/panic and pedantic clippy is denied, so constructors/tests/helpers must use Result/matches/asserts instead of shortcuts.
Public types convention is to derive Debug and document public fields, e.g. ChannelConfig in crates/liminal/src/channel/types.rs:24-33.
No Default implementation should be added for ServerConfig; the design explicitly wants explicit file/env values rather than silent defaults.
scout.approach: Create crates/liminal-server/src/config/types.rs and define `ServerConfig` with `#[derive(Debug, Clone, serde::Deserialize)]` even though R1 only calls out Debug+Clone; Deserialize is needed by R3 and avoids later churn. Add `#[serde(deny_unknown_fields)]` to the struct so TOML strictness works. Use `std::net::SocketAddr` and `std::path::PathBuf`, make fields public, and add doc comments for listen_address, channels, routing_rules, persistence_path, and cluster. Do not add inherent defaults or a `Default` impl; tests can construct explicit sample values.
scout.notes: liminal-server/Cargo.toml currently has toml but not serde, so the implementing dev will need to add `serde = { version = "1", features = ["derive"] }` (or equivalent) despite the brief file list not naming Cargo.toml. Without deriving Deserialize on ServerConfig too, R3 cannot parse a full TOML file into the type tree.

files_changed.note: Added ServerConfig with documented fields for listen_address, channels, routing_rules, persistence_path, and cluster.
Added serde derive dependency required for strict TOML deserialization.
Lockfile updated for the new serde direct dependency.

dev.how: ServerConfig is defined in config/types.rs with public documented fields using SocketAddr, Vec<ChannelDef>, Vec<RoutingRuleDef>, Option<PathBuf>, and Option<ClusterConfig>. It derives Debug, Clone, and serde::Deserialize with deny_unknown_fields, and no Default implementation was added.

checklist.note: ServerConfig now includes the required listen address, channel, routing, persistence, and cluster fields.

stories.note: Operators can express topology and persistence configuration declaratively through the ServerConfig type tree.

requirements.title: Define ChannelDef, RoutingRuleDef, and ClusterConfig sub-types
requirements.spec: ChannelDef SHALL contain: name (String), schema_ref (String), durable (bool). RoutingRuleDef SHALL contain: source_channel (String), target_channel (String), predicate (Option<String>). ClusterConfig SHALL contain: node_name (String), seed_nodes (Vec<SocketAddr>). All sub-types SHALL derive Debug, Clone, and serde::Deserialize for TOML parsing. Sub-types SHALL NOT implement Default.
acceptance: ChannelDef, RoutingRuleDef, and ClusterConfig structs exist in config/types.rs
All three derive Debug, Clone, and serde::Deserialize
None of the three implement Default
ChannelDef has name, schema_ref, and durable fields
ClusterConfig has node_name and seed_nodes fields

files: crates/liminal-server/src/config/types.rs:1-1 — new type definitions should live with ServerConfig, likely in the same file created for R1.
crates/liminal-server/Cargo.toml:11-18 — toml exists but serde derive does not; required for these derives.

crates/liminal/src/channel/types.rs:12-33 — library channel config/mode are richer runtime concepts; server config should remain declarative (`durable: bool`, `schema_ref: String`) per brief, not instantiate ChannelConfig here.
crates/liminal/src/routing/predicate.rs:50-86 — runtime predicate AST exists, but R2 asks for `predicate: Option<String>` so do not parse predicates in config types.
context: All three sub-types must derive Debug, Clone, and serde::Deserialize; derive PartialEq/Eq can help tests but is not required by acceptance.
Apply `#[serde(deny_unknown_fields)]` to ChannelDef, RoutingRuleDef, and ClusterConfig as well as ServerConfig; otherwise unknown keys nested under arrays/tables will be silently ignored.
SocketAddr implements Deserialize via serde when serde is available, so `seed_nodes: Vec<SocketAddr>` can be typed directly.
No Default impls for sub-types; avoid builder APIs that imply omitted values are filled in.
scout.approach: In types.rs define `ChannelDef { name: String, schema_ref: String, durable: bool }`, `RoutingRuleDef { source_channel: String, target_channel: String, predicate: Option<String> }`, and `ClusterConfig { node_name: String, seed_nodes: Vec<SocketAddr> }`, each with public fields and doc comments. Derive `Debug, Clone, serde::Deserialize` and put `#[serde(deny_unknown_fields)]` on each struct. Keep these as declarative DTOs only; mapping to `liminal::channel::ChannelConfig`, schema loading, or routing predicate compilation belongs to later lifecycle work.
scout.notes: TOML examples should use snake_case keys matching Rust field names (`schema_ref`, `source_channel`, `seed_nodes`). If tests need comparisons, deriving PartialEq/Eq is safe for these fields, but do not let extra derives distract from the acceptance requirements.

files_changed.note: Added ChannelDef, RoutingRuleDef, and ClusterConfig as strict declarative DTOs.
Added serde derive dependency needed by these sub-types.

requirements.title: Implement TOML file parser
requirements.spec: A load_from_file function SHALL accept a file path and return Result<ServerConfig, ServerError>. WHEN the file does not exist or is unreadable, THEN it SHALL return ServerError::ConfigLoad with the underlying IO error. WHEN the TOML is malformed or has unknown fields, THEN it SHALL return ServerError::ConfigLoad with the parse error details. The parser SHALL NOT silently ignore unknown fields -- strict deserialization is required.
acceptance: load_from_file(path) exists in crates/liminal-server/src/config/file.rs
Valid TOML parses into a ServerConfig
Missing file returns ServerError::ConfigLoad
Malformed TOML returns ServerError::ConfigLoad with parse details
Unknown fields in TOML cause a parse error, not silent ignoring

files: crates/liminal-server/src/config/file.rs:1-1 — new parser module should expose `load_from_file` and module-local tests.
crates/liminal-server/src/error.rs:1-10 — wrap both fs and toml errors in `ServerError::ConfigLoad { message }`; no source-carrying variant exists.
crates/liminal-server/Cargo.toml:11-18 — has `toml = "0.8"`, missing serde dependency needed by deserialization.
crates/liminal-server/src/server/runtime.rs:14-26 — current run() does not load config; useful for later pipeline integration but R3 itself should not bind/start anything.
docs/design/server/briefs/SRV-002.json:52-68 — exact parser behavior including missing file, malformed TOML, and unknown field failures.
context: Use `std::fs::read_to_string` (or read) then `toml::from_str::<ServerConfig>`; both errors should become ConfigLoad with enough detail for operators.
Strict unknown-field rejection depends on `#[serde(deny_unknown_fields)]` on every config struct, not on toml itself.
There are no existing liminal-server tests; nearby crate tests use module-local `#[cfg(test)] mod tests` and avoid unwrap/expect due workspace lint.
Do not call validation from `load_from_file`; R5 defines the pipeline order and validation belongs after env overlay.
scout.approach: Create `file.rs` with `pub fn load_from_file(path: impl AsRef<Path>) -> Result<ServerConfig, ServerError>` or `&Path` if you want a simpler signature; read the file, map IO errors to `ServerError::ConfigLoad { message: format!("failed to read configuration file '{}': {error}", path.display()) }`, then parse with `toml::from_str` and map parse errors similarly. Add tests with a temporary file strategy that avoids unwrap/expect (for example `std::env::temp_dir()` plus unique filenames and explicit Result-returning test functions) covering valid TOML, missing file, malformed TOML, and an unknown key. Keep malformed/unknown errors as ConfigLoad, not ConfigValidation.
scout.notes: If adding a dev dependency like tempfile would simplify tests, it requires Cargo.toml changes not listed in the brief; the repo currently has no tempfile dependency. Unknown field tests must include unknown nested fields too if you want confidence that all structs have deny_unknown_fields.

files_changed.note: Added load_from_file plus parser tests for valid TOML, missing files, malformed TOML, and unknown fields.
Strict serde deny_unknown_fields on all config structs enables unknown-field rejection.

dev.how: load_from_file reads the supplied path, maps I/O failures to ServerError::ConfigLoad with file context, then deserializes TOML into ServerConfig and maps parse/unknown-field failures to ServerError::ConfigLoad with parser details. Validation is intentionally not run in load_from_file; it is part of the later pipeline.

checklist.note: Configuration can be loaded from the TOML path provided by the server config pipeline.

stories.note: A single TOML file can define the server configuration and be parsed before startup.

requirements.title: Implement environment variable overlay with LIMINAL_ prefix
requirements.spec: An apply_env_overrides function SHALL scan for environment variables with the LIMINAL_ prefix and apply matching values to a ServerConfig. LIMINAL_LISTEN_ADDRESS SHALL override listen_address. LIMINAL_PERSISTENCE_PATH SHALL override persistence_path. LIMINAL_CLUSTER_NODE_NAME and LIMINAL_CLUSTER_SEED_NODES SHALL override the corresponding ClusterConfig fields. The function SHALL NOT fail on absent environment variables -- only present variables trigger overrides.
acceptance: apply_env_overrides(config) exists in crates/liminal-server/src/config/env.rs
LIMINAL_LISTEN_ADDRESS overrides config.listen_address
LIMINAL_PERSISTENCE_PATH overrides config.persistence_path
LIMINAL_CLUSTER_NODE_NAME overrides config.cluster.node_name
Absent environment variables leave config fields unchanged

files: crates/liminal-server/src/config/env.rs:1-1 — new env overlay module should expose `apply_env_overrides` and env-specific parsing helpers/tests.
crates/liminal-server/src/config/types.rs:1-1 — overlay mutates `ServerConfig` and nested `ClusterConfig`; choose function signature around owned vs mutable config deliberately.
crates/liminal-server/src/error.rs:1-10 — parse failures for present env vars need an existing ServerError variant if the function returns Result.
docs/design/server/briefs/SRV-002.json:71-87 — env names and absent-variable behavior.
crates/liminal-server/src/server/runtime.rs:14-26 — current run() only checks empty path; later pipeline can call env overlay here without listener work.
context: The brief says absent env vars must not fail; it does not say malformed present env vars are ignored. To preserve validation-before-startup, present malformed values should return ConfigLoad or ConfigValidation rather than panic.

`listen_address` and `seed_nodes` are already SocketAddr typed; env strings must be parsed before assignment, so `apply_env_overrides` should likely return `Result<ServerConfig, ServerError>` or mutate and return Result.
`LIMINAL_CLUSTER_NODE_NAME` / `LIMINAL_CLUSTER_SEED_NODES` only make sense if `config.cluster` is Some; the spec says override corresponding ClusterConfig fields, not create cluster defaults.
Process env is global; tests that set variables can race under cargo test unless serialized or wrapped carefully.
scout.approach: Implement `apply_env_overrides(mut config: ServerConfig) -> Result<ServerConfig, ServerError>` (or `&mut ServerConfig -> Result<(), ServerError>`) that checks the exact four supported names using `std::env::var_os`/`var`, parses present values, and leaves all fields unchanged when absent. Parse `LIMINAL_LISTEN_ADDRESS` as SocketAddr; convert `LIMINAL_PERSISTENCE_PATH` into `Some(PathBuf::from(value))` (decide whether empty should be a validation error rather than clearing); update nested cluster fields only when `config.cluster.as_mut()` exists. For `LIMINAL_CLUSTER_SEED_NODES`, document and test a delimiter, with comma-separated addresses the most natural container-friendly choice.
scout.notes: There is an acceptance item only for cluster node name, but the spec also requires seed_nodes. If cluster is None and cluster env vars are present, do not silently synthesize a ClusterConfig with empty/default seed_nodes because that violates the no-silent-defaults principle; return a config error or leave a clear test documenting the chosen behavior.

files_changed.note: Added apply_env_overrides and tests for all required LIMINAL_ override variables.

dev.how: apply_env_overrides scans process environment variables for the LIMINAL_ prefix and applies the supported exact keys. It overrides listen_address, persistence_path, cluster.node_name, and comma-separated cluster.seed_nodes when present, leaves absent values unchanged, rejects malformed present socket addresses as ConfigLoad, and rejects cluster overrides without an explicit cluster section as ConfigValidation to avoid silent defaults.

checklist.note: Supported LIMINAL_ environment variables overlay file-loaded configuration values.

stories.note: Container orchestration can inject per-environment values through environment variables.

acceptance.evidence: crates/liminal-server/src/config/env.rs:25 defines pub fn apply_env_overrides(config: ServerConfig) -> Result<ServerConfig, ServerError>.

requirements.title: Enforce environment override precedence over file values
requirements.spec: WHEN both a TOML file and an environment variable specify the same configuration field, THEN the environment variable value SHALL take precedence (P6). The config loading pipeline SHALL be: load from file, then apply env overrides, then validate. The pipeline SHALL NOT reorder these steps or skip the overlay phase.
acceptance: Config pipeline calls load_from_file then apply_env_overrides in that order
A TOML file sets listen_address to 127.0.0.1:8080 and LIMINAL_LISTEN_ADDRESS is set to 0.0.0.0:9090 -- the result is 0.0.0.0:9090
Env override applies after file load but before validation
Test: file value plus env override produces env value in final config

files: crates/liminal-server/src/config/env.rs:1-1 — likely home for a small public pipeline helper or at minimum precedence tests alongside `apply_env_overrides`.
crates/liminal-server/src/config/file.rs:1-1 — first pipeline step must be load_from_file, and tests can reuse valid TOML fixtures from parser tests.
crates/liminal-server/src/config/validation.rs:1-1 — final pipeline step; R5 acceptance requires env overlay before validation.

crates/liminal-server/src/server/runtime.rs:14-26 — eventual runtime integration point; current SRV-001 stub logs and returns without binding, so adding load/overlay/validate here would still respect SRV-002 boundaries if desired.
docs/design/server/briefs/SRV-002.json:90-105 — explicit pipeline order and precedence acceptance test.
context: The brief’s file list says modify env.rs only, but acceptance talks about the config loading pipeline; a helper such as `load(path)` in config/mod.rs would violate CN3, so place pipeline logic in env.rs/file.rs/validation.rs or runtime.rs, not mod.rs.
Environment values must be applied to the already-deserialized config before `validate(&config)`, because validation should see final values and reject bad env overrides.
Do not reorder by validating file first; a file-only invalid listen address could be corrected by LIMINAL_LISTEN_ADDRESS and should pass if env overlay precedes validation.
Tests that use env vars need cleanup/serialization to avoid polluting other tests.
scout.approach: Add an integration-style unit test (probably in env.rs or a public helper module) that writes a TOML config with `listen_address = "127.0.0.1:8080"`, sets `LIMINAL_LISTEN_ADDRESS=0.0.0.0:9090`, calls `load_from_file`, then `apply_env_overrides`, then `validate`, and asserts the final address is `0.0.0.0:9090`. If adding a public pipeline function, name it narrowly (for example `load_config(path)`) and implement it outside mod.rs as `let config = load_from_file(path)?; let config = apply_env_overrides(config)?; validate(&config)?; Ok(config)`. Runtime run() can call that helper without binding a port, but avoid changing server behavior beyond config validation unless the brief runner expects it.
scout.notes: Because `listen_address` is typed as SocketAddr, TOML deserialization will already reject unparseable file addresses as ConfigLoad, making the R5 'file invalid but env corrects it' scenario impossible for parse-level invalidity. The precedence test should use two valid addresses, as acceptance states.

files_changed.note: Added precedence test proving file listen_address is replaced by LIMINAL_LISTEN_ADDRESS before validation.
Added a private load_config helper that executes load_from_file, apply_env_overrides, then validate.
Runtime now invokes the config pipeline and logs the validated listen address without binding it.

dev.how: The implemented pipeline order is file load, then environment overlay, then validation. A unit test writes a TOML listen_address of 127.0.0.1:8080, overlays LIMINAL_LISTEN_ADDRESS as 0.0.0.0:9090 via the injectable env helper, validates, and asserts the final value is the env value. Runtime startup now calls the same private load_config pipeline before any future listener phase.
dev.deviation: The brief file list only named env.rs, but the scouted notes allowed a helper outside mod.rs and runtime integration. I added a private helper in file.rs and called it from runtime.rs so the pipeline is real startup behavior while still preserving the boundary that SRV-002 must not bind a port.

checklist.note: Environment values are applied after file loading and before validation, with tests proving override precedence.

stories.note: Base container config can be overridden by deployment environment variables.

acceptance.criterion: Config pipeline calls load_from_file then apply_env_overrides in that order
acceptance.evidence: crates/liminal-server/src/config/file.rs:32-35 defines load_config with load_from_file(path) at line 33, apply_env_overrides(config) at line 34, then validate(&config) at line 35.

acceptance.criterion: A TOML file sets listen_address to 127.0.0.1:8080 and LIMINAL_LISTEN_ADDRESS is set to 0.0.0.0:9090 -- the result is 0.0.0.0:9090
acceptance.evidence: Test config::env::tests::file_then_env_then_validate_pipeline_gives_env_precedence passed; it loads 127.0.0.1:8080 at crates/liminal-server/src/config/env.rs:303-315, overlays 0.0.0.0:9090 at lines 318-321, and asserts that final address at line 325.

acceptance.criterion: Env override applies after file load but before validation
acceptance.evidence: crates/liminal-server/src/config/file.rs:33-35 orders load_from_file, apply_env_overrides, validate; runtime calls that pipeline before returning at crates/liminal-server/src/server/runtime.rs:22.

acceptance.criterion: Test: file value plus env override produces env value in final config
acceptance.evidence: config::env::tests::file_then_env_then_validate_pipeline_gives_env_precedence passed in cargo test -p liminal-server and asserts the env value at crates/liminal-server/src/config/env.rs:325.

requirements.title: Implement configuration validation with specific error messages
requirements.spec: A validate function SHALL accept a ServerConfig reference and return Result<(), ServerError>. IF listen_address is not a valid socket address, THEN it SHALL return ServerError::ConfigValidation with the field name and reason. IF any channel name is empty or duplicated, THEN it SHALL return ServerError::ConfigValidation. IF persistence_path is Some but the path does not exist or is not writable, THEN it SHALL return ServerError::ConfigValidation. IF cluster seed_nodes is non-empty but contains unparseable addresses, THEN it SHALL return ServerError::ConfigValidation. Validation SHALL collect all errors and report them together, not fail on the first one. Validation SHALL NOT start any I/O beyond filesystem stat checks.
acceptance: validate(config) exists in crates/liminal-server/src/config/validation.rs
Invalid listen_address returns ConfigValidation with field name
Duplicate channel names return ConfigValidation listing the duplicates
Unreachable persistence_path returns ConfigValidation with the path
Multiple validation errors are collected and reported together
Validation performs no network I/O

files: crates/liminal-server/src/config/validation.rs:1-1 — new validation module should collect errors and expose `validate`.
crates/liminal-server/src/error.rs:1-10 — use `ServerError::ConfigValidation { message }` with an aggregated message; no structured list variant exists.

crates/liminal-server/src/config/types.rs:1-1 — because listen_address and seed_nodes are SocketAddr, validation cannot detect parse errors after deserialization except env parse errors handled earlier.
crates/liminal/src/routing/table.rs:168-180 — example of non-panicking helper style using match and returning safe values under strict linting.
docs/design/server/briefs/SRV-002.json:108-125 — required validation cases and 'collect all errors' acceptance.
context: Spec mentions invalid listen_address and unparseable seed_nodes, but typed `SocketAddr` fields mean TOML parse/env overlay should catch unparsable values before validate; validate can still include field names in env parse errors or check semantic constraints if any are chosen.
Duplicate channel detection should preserve deterministic output; use BTreeMap/BTreeSet rather than HashMap if comparing messages in tests.
Persistence path validation permits filesystem stat checks only. Check existence and writability metadata/permissions without creating files or opening network sockets.
Workspace lint `clippy::pedantic = deny` may complain about formatting/collection patterns; keep helpers small and deterministic.
scout.approach: Create `validation.rs` with `pub fn validate(config: &ServerConfig) -> Result<(), ServerError>` that pushes human-readable field-prefixed messages into `Vec<String>` and returns Ok only if empty. Check channel names for empty/whitespace and duplicates; validate routing rules reference known channel names if you choose, but keep it declarative and don’t compile predicates; for `persistence_path`, use `std::fs::metadata` and reject missing paths, non-directories if you treat it as a directory, and readonly permissions via `metadata.permissions().readonly()`; for cluster, reject empty node_name and duplicate seed_nodes if useful. Join all errors with `; ` or newline so multiple independent failures appear in one `ConfigValidation` message.
scout.notes: Do not attempt network reachability checks for listen_address or seed_nodes; no TcpListener bind, TcpStream connect, DNS, or beamr cluster calls. On Unix/macOS, metadata readonly is an imperfect writability test (ACLs may still deny writes), but it satisfies the 'stat checks only' boundary better than creating a probe file.

files_changed.note: Added aggregate semantic validation and tests for listener, channel duplicates, persistence paths, routing references, and multiple errors.

dev.how: validate collects all discovered errors into one ServerError::ConfigValidation message instead of returning on the first failure. It reports listen_address semantic errors with the field name, empty/duplicate channel names deterministically, routing rules that reference unknown channels, unreachable/non-directory/read-only persistence paths via filesystem metadata only, empty cluster node names, duplicate seed nodes, and zero seed-node ports. Because R1/R2 require SocketAddr fields, unparseable listen addresses and seed_nodes are rejected during TOML/env parsing before validate can receive them.

checklist.note: Invalid configurations are rejected with specific aggregated messages before any listener startup; validation performs no network I/O.

stories.note: Operators receive actionable startup validation errors before the server accepts traffic.

acceptance.criterion: validate(config) exists in crates/liminal-server/src/config/validation.rs
acceptance.evidence: crates/liminal-server/src/config/validation.rs:17 defines pub fn validate(config: &ServerConfig) -> Result<(), ServerError>.

acceptance.criterion: Invalid listen_address returns ConfigValidation with field name
acceptance.evidence: crates/liminal-server/src/config/validation.rs:35-38 reports listen_address when the configured port is zero; test config::validation::tests::invalid_listen_address_reports_field_name passed.

acceptance.criterion: Duplicate channel names return ConfigValidation listing the duplicates
acceptance.evidence: crates/liminal-server/src/config/validation.rs:41-60 detects and reports duplicate channel names; test config::validation::tests::duplicate_channel_names_are_listed passed.

acceptance.criterion: Unreachable persistence_path returns ConfigValidation with the path
acceptance.evidence: crates/liminal-server/src/config/validation.rs:95-120 uses filesystem metadata and includes the path in errors; test config::validation::tests::unreachable_persistence_path_reports_path passed.

acceptance.criterion: Multiple validation errors are collected and reported together
acceptance.evidence: crates/liminal-server/src/config/validation.rs:18-31 accumulates all validation errors before returning one ServerError::ConfigValidation; test config::validation::tests::multiple_validation_errors_are_reported_together passed.

acceptance.criterion: Validation performs no network I/O
acceptance.evidence: crates/liminal-server/src/config/validation.rs:20-24 only calls local validation helpers; persistence checks use std::fs::metadata at line 100. Search for TcpListener/bind/connect/UdpSocket in crates/liminal-server/src found no network I/O calls in validation or runtime.

requirements.title: Create config module structure with re-exports
requirements.spec: config/mod.rs SHALL declare pub mod for types, file, env, and validation. config/mod.rs SHALL re-export ServerConfig, ChannelDef, RoutingRuleDef, ClusterConfig, load_from_file, apply_env_overrides, and validate. config/mod.rs SHALL contain ONLY module declarations and re-exports -- no logic (CN3).
acceptance: config/mod.rs declares pub mod types, file, env, validation
config/mod.rs re-exports ServerConfig and the config loading/validation functions
config/mod.rs contains zero function definitions or struct definitions

files: crates/liminal-server/src/config/mod.rs:1-1 — currently empty; replace with module declarations and re-exports only.
crates/liminal-server/src/lib.rs:1-8 — config module is already public, so mod.rs re-exports become part of `liminal_server::config::*`.
crates/liminal-server/src/server/mod.rs:1-3 — existing mod.rs convention: only `pub mod` plus `pub use`, matching CN3.
crates/liminal-server/src/cluster/mod.rs:1-1 — another empty placeholder; do not copy any logic here.
docs/design/server/briefs/SRV-002.json:128-140 — exact re-export list and zero logic requirement.
context: CN3 is already followed in server/mod.rs: a `pub mod runtime;` plus `pub use runtime::run;` and no logic.
config/mod.rs should not define structs, functions, constants, tests, or helper logic; keep tests in submodules/files.
Re-export names required: ServerConfig, ChannelDef, RoutingRuleDef, ClusterConfig, load_from_file, apply_env_overrides, validate.
Ordering can be simple and rustfmt-friendly: declarations first, then grouped pub use lines.
scout.approach: Replace config/mod.rs with `pub mod env; pub mod file; pub mod types; pub mod validation;` followed by `pub use env::apply_env_overrides; pub use file::load_from_file; pub use types::{ChannelDef, ClusterConfig, RoutingRuleDef, ServerConfig}; pub use validation::validate;`. Keep any pipeline helper in a submodule and re-export it only if intentionally public; do not place a function body or test module in mod.rs.
scout.notes: If the dev creates a pipeline helper for R5, avoid putting it in mod.rs even though that file is tempting. A quick grep for `fn ` in config/mod.rs should return nothing.

files_changed.note: Added pub mod declarations and required re-exports only.

dev.how: config/mod.rs now declares env, file, types, and validation modules, and re-exports apply_env_overrides, load_from_file, validate, ServerConfig, ChannelDef, RoutingRuleDef, and ClusterConfig. It contains no function bodies, struct definitions, constants, tests, or other logic.

acceptance.criterion: config/mod.rs declares pub mod types, file, env, validation
acceptance.evidence: crates/liminal-server/src/config/mod.rs:1-4 declares pub mod env, file, types, and validation.

acceptance.criterion: config/mod.rs re-exports ServerConfig and the config loading/validation functions
acceptance.evidence: crates/liminal-server/src/config/mod.rs:6-9 re-exports apply_env_overrides, load_from_file, ServerConfig/ChannelDef/RoutingRuleDef/ClusterConfig, and validate.

acceptance.criterion: config/mod.rs contains zero function definitions or struct definitions
acceptance.evidence: crates/liminal-server/src/config/mod.rs:1-9 contains only pub mod declarations and pub use re-exports; no fn or struct definitions are present.

boundaries: SHALL NOT start the network listener or bind to any port -- that is SRV-003.
SHALL NOT implement runtime channel creation or modification -- channels are defined in config.
SHALL NOT add messaging semantics or channel lifecycle logic -- delegates to liminal library (CN5).
SHALL NOT add silent defaults for required fields -- all config is explicit (P6).
SHALL NOT implement cluster formation or peer discovery -- SRV-005 handles runtime cluster behaviour.
SHALL NOT perform network I/O during validation -- only filesystem stat checks are permitted.
verification: cargo clippy -p liminal-server -- -D warnings passes clean.
Config validation rejects invalid configs before any listener binds (CN6).
Environment variables override file-based config values for the same field.
Multiple validation errors are collected and reported together.
